MARIA N. SOKOVA, Esq., CROWELL & MORING LLP 415.986.2800 msokova@crowell.com Attorneys for Defendants DAVID SMOOKE, LINH DAO SMOOKE, and ARTMAP, INC. d/b/a HACKERNOON

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling with exception of RFP 43 that was modified during oral argument as follows:

DEFENDANTS DAVID SMOOKE AN INDIVIDUAL, LINH SMOOKE, ARTMAP, INC. D/B/A/ HACKERNOON'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Its First Set Of Requests For Document Production, Form Interrogatories, Employment Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories And Complete Privilege Log

Defendants' Motion to Compel Further Responses to its First Set Of Requests For Document Production, Form Interrogatories, Employment Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories And Complete Privilege Log is GRANTED and DENIED as follows. Timeliness: Defendants' Motion is untimely as to SI: 2-4, 10-13, 15-18, and 20-22 and is therefore denied as to these requests. Defendants' reference to Ex. L of Sokova Decl., is not compelling. In that email, Rathjen refers to the 45-day deadline for filing a motion to compel. A notice of motion to compel must be served, if at all, within 45 days after verified responses, or any verified supplemental responses, were served unless the parties agree in writing to extend the time. CCP 2030.300(c). The parties may extend the 45-day limit on making a motion to compel by written agreement specifying a later date. CCP 2030.300(c). Plaintiff's general objections are overruled as improper. The Motion is Granted as to: SI: 5, 23, 24, 25; FI: 210.2, 210.3, 210.4; RFP 26, 43, 48, 93, 95, 96; FI: 8.2, 8.4, 8.5, 8.6, 8.7. The Motion is Denied as to: SI: 14, 26; RFP: 45, FI: 8.1, 8.3. All objections are overruled except attorney-client privilege, attorney work product. If documents are withheld on the basis of either of these privileges, a privilege log shall be served with the responses. Plaintiffs shall provide code-compliant, verified responses and responsive documents (if applicable), including a code-compliant privilege log within three weeks of notice of this order. All requests for sanctions are denied.

Counsel for Defendant is required to submit a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and includes the clarifications discussed during today's oral argument, in compliance with CRC 3.1312, approval as to form, by e-mail to Judge Pro Tem Peter Catalanotti at peter.catalanotti@wilsonelser.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Peter Catalanotti; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/JPT)
